course: Biggs-Kreigh at Cross Creek Golf Club
notes: Most greens are sloped uphill front to back. Pay attention to flag position to avoid downhill putts.

1.description: Short par 5 that doglegs severely left between trees and over a creek following the tee shot.
1.tee_shot: Aim toward the pump house building visible at the end of the fairway — that marks where the hole doglegs downhill left.
hazards: creek at dogleg left
trees lining dogleg
1.green: Sloped back to front. Keep your shot below the hole for best putting opportunity.
1.strategy: Easy birdie opportunity. Reachable in two for longer hitters. Stay left at the dogleg.
1.miss: Missing left of the dogleg leaves you blocked. Right is safer off the tee.

2.description: Long par 3 downhill through trees with tee shot over a pond to a huge undulating green.
2.tee_shot: Carry the pond. The green is large with many different breaks. Men, seniors and ladies tee off from down below.
hazards: pond front — must carry
pond guarding right side of green
2.green: Huge and undulating with many breaks. Par is a good score.
2.strategy: Take enough club to clear the pond. Aim center of the large green — do not chase pins.
2.miss: Missing right is in the pond. Short is in the water. Miss left or long.

3.description: Dead straight par 4 with a downhill second shot to a severely angled green.
3.tee_shot: Play to the middle of the fairway. Straight ahead along Washington Street.
hazards: severely faced green — back to front slope
3.green: Severely angled back to front. Must putt from below the hole to avoid three putts.
3.strategy: Simple tee shot to the middle. The green is the challenge — short side of the flag gives you the best look.
3.miss: Do not go above the hole on this green. Short of the green and chip up is better than over.

4.description: Par 4 dogleg left along high tension lines with homes on both sides.
4.tee_shot: Tee shot favors a draw that bites off the corner. Trees and residences guard the left corner — do not break any windows. Aim to cut the corner.
hazards: trees and homes at the dogleg left corner
mound front right of green
4.green: Good sized mound across the front right half — can hold shots from that direction.
4.strategy: The more of the dogleg corner you can safely cut, the shorter the approach. Conservative play goes straight and leaves longer approach.
4.miss: Left of the dogleg is OB into residences. Stay right of the trees.

5.description: Long par 3 to a large raised green that slopes away front to back.
5.tee_shot: This green is deeper front to back than it is wide. A high tee shot landing short of the flag has the best chance to hold.
hazards: bunker front left
grass bunker right
significant hill slanting away in the back
5.green: Slopes away from the tee front to back. Front pin is much easier than back pin.
5.strategy: Take one extra club and hit high. Land short of the flag and let it release to the pin. Do not go over the back.
5.miss: Long is the worst miss — ball rolls well away down the hill. Miss short or left.

6.description: Short par 4 risk-reward hole. Green is drivable. Pond guards front of the green stopping about 50 yards out.
6.tee_shot: Smart play: hit an iron off the tee favoring the left side, taking the pond out of play, leaving 135 yards or less into the green. Green is drivable for longer hitters but the pond and OB make aggression risky.
hazards: pond across fairway guarding front of green — 50 yards out
bunker front right of green
severe downhill hill behind green — can roll OB
6.green: Going over the back faces a severe downward hill that can allow ball to run OB.
6.strategy: Lay up with iron left of the pond for 100-135 in. Going for the green requires precise carry. Eagles have been made but so have big numbers.
6.lay_up_target: Left side of fairway, leaving 135 yards or less to the green
6.miss: Missing over the back is likely OB. Pond is the primary danger. Miss left of the green.

7.description: Beautiful par 3 across a pond to a raised green.
7.tee_shot: Must carry the pond. Green is quite flat — high trajectory needed to hold. Do not go over the back.
hazards: pond — full carry required
grass bunker front
sand bunkers front left and rear right
severe down slope behind green that typically means OB
7.green: Quite flat. High trajectory shot needed to hold. If you hit over the green hit a provisional — it likely rolls OB.
7.strategy: Take enough club to clear the pond but not so much you go over. The penalty for going long is severe.
7.miss: Going over the back is almost certainly OB. Miss short or in the front bunkers — that is the better play.

8.description: Number one handicap. Long par 5 that usually plays into the wind. Tee shot over water.
8.tee_shot: Tee shot is across water to a fairway lined with trees along the right side and mounds that can kick balls OB. A second pond lives on the left closer to the green for pulled second or third shots.
hazards: water off the tee — must carry
trees and OB mounds right
pond left closer to green
fairway bunker right
greenside bunker front right
8.green: Large and tiered — lower front left and higher back right. Pay attention to flag position — treacherous putts possible.
8.strategy: Lay up short of the left pond on the second shot if needed. You earn your score on the approach. Do not chase the green in two if the pond is in play.
8.miss: Pulled shots left find the second pond. Right finds trees and mounds. Stay center to left center.

9.description: After the hard 8th, this risk-reward par 4 frequently plays downwind.
9.tee_shot: Many golfers choose 3 wood or long iron to leave 100 yards in, avoiding the fairway bunkers and pond along the full left side. Longer hitters can go for the green. Birdies and eagles have been recorded.
hazards: pond along full left side of fairway extending to front of green
fairway bunkers right
crowned green with quick putts
9.green: Crowned and deceitful — putts run quick. Pay attention.
9.strategy: Lay up to 100 yards with an iron if you want to play safe. Driver is high risk high reward with the pond running the full left side.
9.miss: Left is in the pond the entire length of the hole. Miss right on every shot.

10.description: Short straightaway par 4, the easiest hole on the back nine. Broad fairway with little trouble.
10.tee_shot: Wide open. Hit it straight. The ponds to the left really are not in play. Mounds right can be avoided.
hazards: pond along left side of green — not really in play off the tee
mounding right of fairway
10.green: Quite wide open with a pond along left side.
10.strategy: Easiest hole on the course. Take your birdie chance and move on. Any club off the tee works.
10.miss: Hard to miss here. Stay away from the left pond near the green on your approach.

11.description: Downhill par 3 that not unusually has wind to your back.
11.tee_shot: Play downhill with possible tailwind — club down. Avoid going over the green. Missing left goes in the pond almost 100% of the time.
hazards: pond left of green — any miss left is a penalty
steep descent behind green — chip back up is very difficult
11.green: Slopes well away to the rear. Going over leaves a precarious chip back up. Always results in higher numbers.
11.strategy: Club down for the downhill and tailwind. Short or right is the only safe miss. Never go left.
11.miss: Left is in the pond. Over the back leaves an impossible chip. Miss short or right.

12.description: Par 5 with trees left and OB right off the tee. Pond runs the entire left side of the second half of the fairway.
12.tee_shot: Keep tee shot straight away and right of the large poplar tree. OB right into residential area — stay left of the cart path. Some players use 3 wood to avoid trouble.
hazards: OB right into residential — stay left of cart path
pond in left rough reachable by long hitters
pond runs entire left side of fairway second half
pond protects front left of green
12.green: Difficult to reach in two. Pond protects front left. Miss right.
12.strategy: Conservative play: 3 wood off the tee, then layup right of the pond, wedge in. Green in two requires precise second shot over the pond.
12.miss: OB right off the tee. Pond left on second and third shots. Miss right on every approach.

13.description: Slightly uphill par 3 running beside Washington Street.
13.tee_shot: Slightly uphill. Green guarded by a large mound front right and shallow grass bunker left.
hazards: mound front right — will stop low shots
grass bunker left side
13.green: Mound across front right third stops low hit short shots.
13.strategy: Hit a high shot to carry the front mound if the pin is back. Front left of the green is most accessible.
13.miss: Miss left into the grass bunker rather than right into the mound.

14.description: Risk-reward favorite on the course. Short par 4 dogleg left with pond and creek.
14.tee_shot: Conservative: middle iron straight ahead over the pond, landing short of trees and creek on the left. This sets up second shot with low to middle iron, between trees, across creek, uphill to green. Aggressive: Driver over the trees down the left side for eagle or birdie chance.
hazards: pond off the tee
creek across the left side at the dogleg
creek on second shot — must carry uphill
trees on both sides of second shot
14.green: Fairly flat with subtle breaks. Mounds right and behind.
14.strategy: Most players: iron off the tee across the pond, short of the creek. Sets up a short iron in. Big hitters: driver over the corner trees for eagle opportunity.
14.miss: Tee shot: stay right of the creek. Second shot: must carry the creek — there is no safe miss short.

15.description: Direct par 5. Tree line right tee to green. Pond left of the fairway.
15.tee_shot: Drive left center. Trees down the right side grab any push. Pond off the left in a perfect spot for a pull hook.
hazards: trees right side entire length
pond left side off the tee — pull hook danger
bunkers front left and right of green
15.green: Green slightly to the right side of the fairway. Bunkers front left and right.
15.strategy: Left center tee shot sets up second shot. Big hitters can go for the green in two. Three shot strategy: layup short of the bunkers, wedge in.
15.miss: Right is in the trees. Left off the tee is in the pond. Miss left of the green rather than short right into the bunker.

16.description: Challenging par 3 to a raised faced green. Swing King hole — hole in one wins $2500.
16.tee_shot: Short is better than long on this hole. Raised green with significant drop off for long tee balls.
hazards: small bunker left
small bunker right
significant slanted drop off long — ball runs well away
16.green: Raised and angled back to front. Long balls run significantly away from the hole.
16.strategy: Take one less club than you think. Short or in a bunker is far better than over the back.
16.miss: Going over the back is the worst miss — ball runs very far. Miss short into either bunker.

17.description: Par 4 with tee shot over a pond. Grass lined ditch crosses the fairway.
17.tee_shot: Carry the pond off the tee — pond runs half way down the right side. A grass lined ditch crosses the fairway and can catch drives that are just a bit short, leaving a downhill or uphill lie. Favor the left side.
hazards: pond right off the tee
grass ditch crossing the fairway — catches short drives
large faced bunker front right of green
17.green: Large faced bunker protects front right.
17.strategy: Favor the left side to open up approach options and avoid the front right bunker. Do not come up short off the tee into the ditch.
17.miss: Tee: right is in the pond, short is in the ditch. Approach: miss left of the bunker.

18.description: Fun finishing par 5 back to the clubhouse. Forced carry over creek. Dogleg right.
18.tee_shot: Forced carry over a creek. Tee shot lands at the corner of the dogleg right. Long hitters can cut the corner over trees to the right. Typical tee shot is hard left center — ball rolls right off the hill back to the fairway. Do not hit too far left or you are blocked by trees for the second shot.
hazards: creek forced carry off the tee
trees down the left side from dogleg
creek runs along right side near the green
creek bends in front right of green
bunker front left of green
18.green: Small green. Creek right side, bunker front left.
18.strategy: Tee shot left center off the hill. Second shot: trees left, creek right near the green — be precise. Safe layup short of the creek, then wedge over the bunker.
18.miss: Tee: too far left is blocked by trees. Too far right cuts the corner but needs to carry the trees. Approach: creek right, bunker front left — land on the green.
